notes: Between-spike one-shot choice events (backlog §2 P2). The W6/W9-12/W13-14 pressure curve is the spine; these fire in the quiet windows (W4-5 · days 22-35, W7-8 · days 43-56) so mid-summer weeks aren't pure routing. Each event fires at most once per save, at most one event per day, ~30% chance per eligible day. Effects vocabulary (interpreted by _apply_spike_effects): escalation {region: float delta}, spawn_problem [region_id, template_id], queue_burn {days, reason}, log str. Template ids must exist in problems.json; region ids in regions.json. Recruitment events (windows W10-14 · days 64-96) use the agent_arrives effect: the choice IS the roster decision; decline and they never join this save.

events.title: A RESIDENT PETITION
events.body: Forty-one signatures, delivered to the office window by a nine-year-old deputized for the errand. The petition concerns the pool hours, the retention pond smell, and 'the general feeling lately.' The signatures are real. The general feeling is too.

choices.label: Read every name aloud at the office window, then post the reply the same afternoon

effects.log: You read all forty-one names. Word gets around that the office reads its mail. The general feeling improves by roughly one pool afternoon.

choices.label: File it. The summer is long and the pool opens either way

queue_burn.reason: the petition resurfaces at the county desk · forty-one names and a note in a nine-year-old's hand

effects.log: You file it under things the summer will absorb. The summer files you under things it will remember.

events.title: MOTH MAKES AN OFFER
events.body: Moth finds you between dispatches, which Moth does not do. 'Graustark is loud this month. I can make it quiet for a while. You know my rate.' You do not, in fact, know Moth's rate. That is the rate.

choices.label: Accept. Quiet now is worth an unnamed later

queue_burn.reason: Moth's rate comes due · something in Graustark has been missing a name for twelve days and someone finally asks about it

effects.log: Graustark goes quiet the way a room goes quiet when someone very polite enters it. You have twelve days of it.

choices.label: Decline. Nothing with an unstated price

effects.log: Moth nods, unbothered, and returns to the roster. The offer refused keeps a faint hum, like a phone off the hook two rooms away.

events.title: A THREAD GOES HOT
events.body: Overnight, a BACKCHANNEL thread about the Small Wood tower picks up eleven replies, which for BACKCHANNEL is a stadium crowd. Half the posters have seen the window. The other half have seen the first half posting at 3 AM.

choices.label: Post the calm version of the truth, once, and log off

effects.log: You post four sentences, none of them exciting, all of them true. The thread cools the way threads do when someone brings minutes to a meeting.

choices.label: Let it burn out on its own · the board polices itself

effects.log: The thread burns three more nights. On the fourth, two of the posters drive up the ridge road to see for themselves.

events.title: MACKENZIE PROPOSES AN OPEN HOUSE
events.body: Mackenzie wants a Saturday open house at the model home. 'People calm down inside a clean kitchen. It is a fact of the species.' The punch bowl, the sign-in sheet, the good pens · Mackenzie has already priced all of it.

choices.label: Approve it. A calm Saturday is cheap at twice the price

queue_burn.reason: the open-house accounts land · punch bowl, folding chairs, and one good pen that never came back

effects.log: Saturday goes beautifully. Forty residents tour a kitchen none of them will buy and every one of them leaves calmer than they came.

choices.label: Not this month. The calendar is carrying enough

effects.log: Mackenzie takes it professionally, which is somehow worse. The model home's kitchen stays clean for nobody.

events.title: STARLING ASKS TO MOLT IN THE OPEN
events.body: Starling's next evolution is due, and Starling would prefer to do it on the green, at noon, where people can see. 'Hiding it costs me a week. Showing it costs you a story.' Starling has clearly rehearsed the line.

choices.label: Let it happen on the green · spectacle relocates attention

effects.log: The molt draws a crowd of thirty and one unattended zoom lens. For a full week, everyone talks about Starling and nobody talks about Graustark.

choices.label: Behind the substation, after dark, like always

effects.log: Starling molts at 2 AM behind the substation, unwatched, and is a week slower for it. 'Noted,' Starling says, newly iridescent, and lets it go.

events.title: A CALL FROM THE COUNTY PAPER
events.body: A reporter from the county paper calls the office. She is writing a piece about 'the planned community that stays so calm,' and she says the word calm the way people say a word they intend to investigate.

choices.label: Give the interview · boring on the record is armor

queue_burn.reason: the article prints · WHERE SUMMER BEHAVES · and for one weekend the lots have visitors with cameras

effects.log: You give her forty minutes of zoning history and pool maintenance. She thanks you with the flat voice of a person who found nothing, which was the entire performance.

choices.label: Decline politely · no comment is a comment, but a quiet one

effects.log: You decline with the warmth of a man declining seconds at a church supper. The piece runs two column inches on page nine. Nobody in it is quoted.

events.title: RUTH CLOSES THE ARCHIVE EARLY
events.body: Ruth from the county archive appears at the office at 4:51 PM with a cardboard box of annotated folders that are, on inspection, about you. 'I have watched this operation all summer through its paperwork. You are three weeks from drowning and your filing is a crime scene. I can help. Two conditions: I work alone, and no job with one of THOSE on it.' She does not gesture at anything when she says THOSE. She doesn't need to.

choices.label: Take her on · brilliance on her terms

effects.log: Ruth requisitions the good desk by Thursday. Competence 1.8 · the best on the board · but she carries obligation double and closes the desk at three. Spend her like the last of something.

choices.label: Decline · the roster works mixed crews, and that won't bend

effects.log: Ruth nods once, repacks the box, and leaves the annotated folders anyway. 'For when you drown.' The folders are, infuriatingly, useful.

events.title: DEPUTY ROYCE, RETIRED, WAVES YOU DOWN
events.body: Royce flags you from his porch with the two-finger wave of a man who has flagged down four thousand vehicles. 'Heard you're shorthanded. I'm not fast and I'm not clever, but I've been to every meeting this county ever held and I have never once been unavailable.' He waits. He is very good at waiting.

choices.label: Bring him on · never-unavailable is a skill

effects.log: Royce shows up the next morning nine minutes early with his own clipboard. Competence 0.9 and slow to travel, but a cap of seven · he will still be picking up in the storm weeks when the specialists have gone home.

choices.label: Pass · the board needs edge, not floor

effects.log: 'Fair enough,' Royce says, entirely unoffended, and goes back to the porch. He waves you down again in September just to say the tomatoes did fine.

events.title: LANTERN APPLIES, LOUDLY
events.body: A new demon is waiting in the office at dawn, already glowing, already talking. 'I'm Lantern. I'm the fastest thing on this coast and everything I do works. Full disclosure, which is the only kind I have: everyone always sees me do it.' Somewhere outside, a dog is already barking at the office window.

choices.label: Sign Lantern · speed now, headlines later

effects.log: Lantern is dispatched, succeeds, and is photographed, in roughly that order, by Friday. Speed 1.4 and real competence · but burn runs double, corruption resistance is low, and a Lantern failure is a PRESS failure.

choices.label: Turn Lantern away · this operation runs quiet

effects.log: Lantern takes it gracefully at maximum volume. 'YOUR LOSS. NO HARD FEELINGS. I'M LIKE THIS AT ALL TIMES.' The dog outside barks for another full minute.
